canonical_override_exceptions.override_reason:
ZIP 90056 covers two adjacent neighborhoods with split jurisdiction:
Ladera Heights (unincorporated LA County, the larger portion) and Ladera
(City of LA, Council District 11, the smaller portion east of La Cienega
Boulevard). The City of LA's own Zip Codes Listing
(https://finance.lacity.gov/sites/g/files/wph1721/files/2026-04/a_Zip-Codes-Listing-City-of-Los-Angeles.pdf)
lists 90056 under "Baldwin Hills" / "Crenshaw" — the City explicitly
claims jurisdiction over part of the ZIP. The construction's area_ratio
placed 90056 in the `out` bucket per the 0.90/0.10 three-bucket rule
(§2.2-b) with ratio 0.0591 (~5.9% City-side, ~2.6M sq ft ≈ 0.093 sq mi,
consistent with the small Ladera neighborhood east of La Cienega). A
clean `out` verdict would cause the runtime chain (§6.1) to gate-close
on City-side addresses within Ladera before reaching the parcel rail.
This override forces 90056 to the `straddler` bucket so the runtime
chain routes 90056 lookups through the parcel-rail step (§6.1 step-3)
for per-address resolution, which is the correct A-1 disposition for
cross-jurisdictional ZIPs.
canonical_override_exceptions.override_authority:
Broker ruling 2026-06-27, applied at first-construction. Authored under
this A-3 determination §5.4-c (canonical_override_exceptions). The
override addresses a known structural property of 90056 (split
City/Unincorporated jurisdiction with the City-side area below the
0.10 in-threshold) rather than a defect in the construction or in C-8.
Empirical corroboration: the 5.9% City-side share matches the published
jurisdictional split (City "Ladera" east of La Cienega, smaller than
unincorporated "Ladera Heights" to the west).
canonical_override_exceptions.override_scope:
This override applies only to ZIP 90056 and only to the bucket
assignment. It does not modify the area_ratio, the C-8 input, the
ZCTA-2010 input, or any other construction parameter. It is a
post-classification routing override.
canonical_override_exceptions.review_cadence:
Re-evaluated at every snapshot refresh. If a future C-8 boundary edit
changes the City-side share of 90056 such that the area_ratio crosses
into [0.10, 0.90), the override SHALL be removed (the construction's
natural straddler classification supersedes the override). If the
City-side share is eliminated by a future annexation/de-annexation,
the override SHALL be removed and 90056 returned to the natural `out`
bucket. Review is mechanical against the area_ratio at each refresh;
no separate broker attestation is required unless the area_ratio
crosses a threshold.

counts.note: out=35 counts city-intersecting ZCTAs (inter>0) classified unambiguous-out; the full Census ZCTA-2010 California input additionally contains 1615 non-intersecting ZCTAs (ratio 0), so total out rows = 1650. authoritative_total = in + straddler = 119.
